Package edu.uky.cs.nil.sabre.prog

Class GraphHeuristic.AdmissibleMaxGraphHeuristic

java.lang.Object
edu.uky.cs.nil.sabre.prog.GraphHeuristic
edu.uky.cs.nil.sabre.prog.GraphHeuristic.MaxGraphHeuristic
edu.uky.cs.nil.sabre.prog.GraphHeuristic.AdmissibleMaxGraphHeuristic
All Implemented Interfaces:
ProgressionCost
Enclosing class:
GraphHeuristic
public static class GraphHeuristic.AdmissibleMaxGraphHeuristic extends GraphHeuristic.MaxGraphHeuristic
A max graph heuristic that calculates an admissible lower bound on the cost of raising the node's character's utility. While this heuristic is likely to be be an underestimate, if the cost exceeds the numbers of actions remaining based on the search's temporal limit, this function will return Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY, potentially allowing it to prune more nodes than some other heuristics.
Author:
Stephen G. Ware

    • AdmissibleMaxGraphHeuristic

      public AdmissibleMaxGraphHeuristic(MaxGraph graph)
      Constructs a new admissible max graph heuristic from a given max graph.
      Parameters:
      graph - the max graph to use for calculating costs

    • AdmissibleMaxGraphHeuristic

      public AdmissibleMaxGraphHeuristic(CompiledProblem problem, Worker.Status status)
      Constructs a new admissible max graph heuristic and its max graph.
      Parameters:
      problem - that problem for which this heuristic will approximate costs
      status - a status to update while building the max graph

    • evaluate

      public <N> double evaluate(ProgressionNode<N> node)
      Description copied from interface: ProgressionCost
      Returns the cost of a given node based on its context in the progression search that generated it.
      Specified by:
      evaluate in interface ProgressionCost
      Overrides:
      evaluate in class GraphHeuristic
      Type Parameters:
      N - the type of object used to represent a node in the search's search space
